TextENGNew: The days I spent could not, by any stretch, be called comfortable.
changeReason: Old says 'beginning to suffocate me'; new says 'could not be called comfortable'; Japanese 窮屈でないとは言えない is milder.

TextENGNew: I couldn't freely walk outside during the day, and even going to my part-time job required prior arrangements with Mion.
I had believed that escaping the academy would bring a fresh start, some kind of nourishment to my life... For someone who'd clung to that belief, the reality was almost unbearable.
changeReason: Old condenses to 'life wouldn't improve'; new preserves the belief in escape bringing nourishment and reality being unbearable.

TextENGNew: When you live like this for too long, you don't drift toward anything good.
changeReason: Old says 'not healthy'; new says 'you don't drift toward anything good'; Japanese いい方には流れない means drifting negatively.

TextENGNew: To begin with, I was severely lacking in basic human interaction.@k...At this rate, I'd turn into nothing more than a shut-in.
changeReason: Major correction: old says 'no friends, felt like hibernating'; new correctly translates as lacking human interaction and becoming a shut-in (引き篭もり).

TextENGNew: I had begun to harbor a quiet dread that this indolent life was slowly killing me.
changeReason: Old says 'dangers of boredom'; new says 'quiet dread that this life was slowly killing me'; Japanese 緩慢に殺される means being slowly killed.

TextENGNew: Whenever my thoughts drifted, only one face came to mind: Sis's friend, 'Houjou Satoshi-kun.'
changeReason: Old omits 'Sis's friend' (お姉の友人) relationship detail.

TextENGNew: The time we'd actually spent together was unbelievably brief.
changeReason: Old says 'met him briefly'; new adds 'unbelievably' matching Japanese 信じられないほど.

TextENGNew: ...And yet, it was the most vivid experience I'd had since starting my new life in this town.
changeReason: Old says 'great impact'; new says 'most vivid experience'; Japanese 一番鮮烈 means most vivid.

TextENGNew: It hinted at something new -- a new life, a new beginning... To put it in childish terms, it gave me this fluttery, excited feeling.
The more I dwelled on that feeling, the more time I spent thinking about him.
...Perhaps this, too, was a kind of one-sided crush.
changeReason: Old says 'might be a crush'; new says 'one-sided crush'; Japanese 片思い specifically means unrequited.

TextENGNew: I couldn't help but smile wryly at my own shallowness, and at the maiden-like way I was romanticizing his image.
changeReason: Old omits the self-deprecating 'shallowness' and 'maiden-like' details from Japanese.

TextENGNew: Then one day, just like that,@kI got the chance to see him again.
As I passed by a park, I spotted a familiar figure sitting on the swings, lost in twilight reverie.
Was he spacing out, or was he absorbed in thought?
...Even after I walked right up in front of him, he didn't notice me for a while.
I reminded myself several times -- I am Sonozaki Mion, I need to talk like Mion -- before calling out.
changeReason: New preserves the repeated self-talk 'I am Mion, talk like Mion' matching Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``Hey there.@k@vS06/06/150600362. What're you doing in a place like this?``
``You don't look very startled.@k@vS06/06/150600364. I've been standing here a while. You really didn't notice?``
``...Really?@k@vS06/07/151200036. Sorry, I didn't see you.``
He gave a faint, sheepish smile and a halfhearted shrug.
The way he looked so plainly drained of energy was painful to see.@kOne glance was enough to tell something was weighing on him.
changeReason: Old says 'He looked depressed'; new expands to match Japanese: drained of energy, painful to see.

TextENGNew: ``...Something on your mind?@k@vS06/06/150600366. If talking about it would help, I'm all ears. And if there's anything I can do, just say the word.``
``No, it's nothing...``
changeReason: Old says 'that's okay'; new says 'it's nothing'; Japanese 別に means nothing special.

TextENGNew: ``I owe you one from before, Satoshi-kun.@k@vS06/06/150600368. Or what,@k@vS06/06/150600369. you don't think this old man here can help?@k@vS06/06/150600370. Don't sell me short now.``
changeReason: Old says 'how mighty I am'; new says 'Don't sell me short'; Japanese 見くびられた means underestimated.

TextENGNew: ``It's fine...@k@vS06/07/151200039. ...It's the kind of thing that borrowing your strength wouldn't fix, Mion.``
changeReason: Old says 'You don't have to help'; new says 'your help wouldn't fix it'; Japanese says Mion's help would be meaningless.

TextENGNew: ``...Oh?@k@vS06/06/150600372. Meaning what, exactly?@k@vS06/06/150600373. You're saying a mere girl like me isn't good enough to help?``
Annoyed by his vaguely unsatisfying refusal, I puffed out my cheeks in protest.
...Satoshi-kun quickly noticed he'd given the wrong impression and softened his expression to correct himself.
changeReason: Old says 'noticed and smiled'; new says 'noticed wrong impression and softened expression'; much closer to Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``That's not what I mean, Mion.@k@vS06/07/151200041. ...It's just... I didn't have the skill... I couldn't come through when it mattered most, that's all.``
changeReason: Old says 'messed up my swing'; new says 'didn't have the skill'; Japanese 実力がなくて means lacking ability.

TextENGNew: ...I rapidly searched through the files of information Sis had given me, pulling up everything tagged under 'Satoshi-kun.'
...Despite his bookish appearance, Satoshi-kun was actually on a youth baseball team.
changeReason: Old says 'doesn't look like an athlete'; new says 'bookish appearance'; Japanese 文系 means literary type.

TextENGNew: The team was called the 'Hinamizawa Fighters.'
Given the total absence of stories about his exploits, I estimated his skill level was low.@kPerhaps embarrassed by that, he didn't seem to broadcast his membership very widely.
But the dejection he showed at failing to meet expectations suggested he had real passion for it...
I took that murky soup of data, ran it through the filter of 'how would Sis say this,' and spoke...
changeReason: New preserves Japanese food metaphor of murky soup of data and the Mion filter concept.

TextENGNew: ``Is that all...?@k@vS06/06/150600375. You're moping over something like that?``
``'Something like that,' she says.@k@vS06/07/151200043. ...You make it sound so easy, Mion.``
changeReason: Old says 'not easy to get over'; new says 'you make it sound easy'; Japanese is about speaking tone, not recovery difficulty.

TextENGNew: Laughing breezily, I slapped him on the back with cheerful tactlessness.
changeReason: Old says 'pat his back'; new says 'slapped with cheerful tactlessness'; Japanese バンバンと is vigorous.

TextENGNew: Satoshi-kun would probably respond with something like 'easy for you to say'...
When someone is genuinely troubled, it's usually better to listen seriously rather than blundering in with careless encouragement.
This 'Sis' response was probably the wrong call... but right now I was playing Sis, so even knowing it was a misstep, I had to follow through.@kThis is simply how Sis would handle it.

``Ah ha ha ha.@k@vS06/06/150600377. Instead of sulking, you should throw yourself into practice even harder and aim for the next chance! Right?@k@vS06/06/150600378. At times like these, all you can do is practice, practice, practice!``
``Well, if you're so obsessed with beating the other pitcher that you'd be willing to play a little dirty, then this old man might be able to help in all sorts of ways.``
``Mmph... There's no point if I cheat...@k@vS06/07/151200045. ...Hahhh...``
Satoshi-kun exhaled his lingering regrets in one big breath and hung his head.
Surrender. Resignation. Oh well, nothing to be done.@k...That was the gesture.
``Can't tell if you're quick to give up or just clinging to regret.@k@vS06/06/150600381. But since you were born a man, don't you want to be the star of the big stage at least once?``
changeReason: Old says 'persistent'; new says 'clinging to regret'; Japanese 未練たらたら means dripping with lingering regret.

TextENGNew: Satoshi-kun answered with a grin that said 'well, of course.'
``Sure, being the hero is the dream.@k@vS06/07/151200047. But that's not why I play baseball.``
``I play because I genuinely enjoy throwing and hitting the ball.@k@vS06/07/151200049. ...Even you can understand that, right, Mion?``
changeReason: Old omits Mion's name from the question; new correctly includes it.

TextENGNew: ``Hmm, this old man doesn't really get that kind of thing.@k@vS06/06/150600383. If the result is coming in first, I'll love any sport.@k@vS06/06/150600384. But loving something you can't be the best at? That I don't really understand.@k@vS06/06/150600385. Ahahaha!``
changeReason: Old says 'I only love games I can win'; new says 'If first place, I'll love any sport'; closer to Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``Ahahahahahahahaha. That's such a Mion answer.``
changeReason: Old splits dialogue incorrectly between speakers; new correctly formats.

TextENGNew: Satoshi-kun realized I was laughing to cheer him up and joined in to match my mood.@k...Judging by how refreshed his smile looked, he couldn't have been that depressed to begin with.
So basically, Satoshi-kun had been sulking because he'd grounded out at a critical moment in the game.
changeReason: Old says 'feeling down because of game'; new says 'sulking because he grounded out'; Japanese 凡退 is a specific baseball term.

TextENGNew: He wasn't as down as I'd thought.
He'd just been sitting in the park having a drink after the game, and I'd mistaken his idle relaxation for brooding. A total misread on my part.
changeReason: New adds he was having a drink and she mistook relaxation for brooding, details in Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``...But you know, Mion, you always put in the effort to come out on top.@k@vS06/07/151200053. I think that's really admirable.@k@vS06/07/151200054. Yeah.``
Satoshi-kun smiled softly, then rose from the swing and... gently stroked my head.@kCompletely out of nowhere.@kCatching me with my guard down.
This happened last time too, but...
changeReason: New preserves kindergarten reference and switch-flipping metaphor from Japanese.

TextENGNew: ...Why does this boy pat people on the head so casually...?!

Nobody had stroked my head since kindergarten. Normally.@kLike a switch being flipped, a flood of childhood memories came rushing back... and I was seized by the illusion of shrinking back into a little girl.
...My face went scarlet, embarrassment welling up... and this warm, fuzzy feeling taking over...
changeReason: Old says 'blushing with eyes closed'; new adds 'all I could do was squeeze eyes shut until done.'

TextENGNew: Satoshi-kun did it as naturally and carelessly as if he were petting a cat.
Meanwhile, all I could do was squeeze my eyes shut and go beet-red until he was done.
Just like a girl in a dentist's chair, mouth open and eyes clenched shut, terrified of what comes next.
changeReason: Old says 'I hate him'; new says 'infuriating he had no idea of his effect'; Japanese 憎々しい is annoyance, not literal hatred.

TextENGNew: ...Completely oblivious to how I felt, the person doing the stroking was as cool and composed as could be.
...It was infuriating that he had absolutely no idea what effect he was having on me.
I could only close my eyes and endure those few seconds until Satoshi-kun finished.@kThen, abruptly, his hand released me.
``...Well, I should head home.@k@vS06/07/151200056. My aunt asked me to pick up groceries on the way back from baseball.``
My voice came out in this high-pitched, embarrassing squeak.@k...What kind of sound was I even making...?
``I-I mean, um!@k@vS06/06/150600390. ...I'm heading out to shop too.@k@vS06/06/150600391. ...For dinner groceries.``
changeReason: Old says 'some pride'; new says 'masculine pride'; Japanese 男としての specifies 'as a man.'

TextENGNew: ``Huh?@k@vS06/07/151200059. Wait.@k@vS06/07/151200060. You do your own shopping, Mion?@k@vS06/07/151200061. I thought the servants took care of all that.``
Ugh...@kI slipped up.
The main Sonozaki house left all the cooking and provisioning to their staff.
changeReason: Old says 'bragged'; new says 'shared'; Japanese 公開 means disclosed.

TextENGNew: Mion herself almost never went grocery shopping, and she'd shared that tidbit with certain people.@k...I'd fumbled a line as 'Mion.' A rare mistake for either of us.
But covering for it wouldn't be too difficult.

``I was planning to come into town anyway, so I figured I'd try my hand at housework for once.@k@vS06/06/150600393. So I volunteered to do the shopping.@k@vS06/06/150600394. Got a problem with that?``
``...Ahaha.@k@vS06/07/151200063. That's unusual for you, Mion.@k@vS06/07/151200064. You usually say that kind of thing is a pain and avoid it.``
``That's what we call 'none of your business'!``
changeReason: Old says 'You talk too much!'; new says 'none of your business!'; Japanese 余計なお世話 means unnecessary meddling.

TextENGNew: ``...Ahh!@k@vS06/06/150600397. Stop petting my head~!``
``Ahaha, ahaha.``
Satoshi-kun seemed to find my flustered reactions amusing, because he kept stroking my head for a good while.
But I managed to fast-talk my way through it and survive the moment.@kSatoshi-kun apparently had the convenient personality of not sweating the small stuff...
Walking alongside Satoshi-kun through the Kami-isshiki shopping district... a thought occurred to me.
What had that slip-up actually been about?
Mion never goes grocery shopping, and yet I'd claimed I was out shopping too. Such a careless error.
...Had I made a mistake while acting as Mion?
No... it was probably the opposite.@kI'd spoken my own words, unfiltered, without running them through the Mion mask.
I can't lie to myself.@k...So I accepted it without overthinking.
What it came down to was this:@k...I simply wanted to spend as much time with Satoshi-kun as I possibly could... That's all it was.
changeReason: New preserves the chick imprinting metaphor from Japanese.

TextENGNew: When I thought about why I was this drawn to him, like a chick imprinted on the first thing it sees, the answer came easily enough.
He was a novel encounter in the midst of my monotonous days.

There was the ease of our relationship, since he mistook me for Mion.@kEt cetera, et cetera.@k...And at the very bottom of the list, I'd add: because he's the kind of person who casually pats people on the head.
Lumping all of that together... ahh, I decided I was simply enjoying what people call a faint stirring of love.
Yes, that's all. I was just casually savoring a crush.
It wasn't that I'd actually fallen in love with him.
I was merely enjoying a bit of time with someone of the opposite sex.
Once I framed it that way, the idle chatter and the time spent walking beside Satoshi-kun felt genuinely, honestly fun.
``Umm...@k@vS06/07/151200067. ...Which one's broccoli again?@k@vS06/07/151200068. ...Is it the green one?@k@vS06/07/151200069. Or the yellow one?``
``Satoshi-kun, that's cauliflower.@k@vS06/06/150600399. ...Don't tell me you're the type who can't tell cabbage from lettuce, either?``
``H-Hey, that's harsh.@k@vS06/07/151200071. I'm doing my best to tell them apart here...``
Despite his protests, Satoshi-kun was locked in a staring contest with the piles of broccoli and cauliflower.
After agonizing over it, his hand drifted, as if magnetically drawn, toward the wrong one...
``That's not it!@k@vS06/06/150600401. ...Watching you shop is nerve-wracking, Satoshi-kun.@k@vS06/06/150600402. Entertaining for me, though.@k@vS06/06/150600403. Ahahahaha!``
changeReason: Old says 'roller coaster'; new says 'nerve-wracking'; Japanese ハラハラ means anxious.

TextENGNew: ``...Mmph.@k@vS06/07/151200073. You've got me there.@k@vS06/07/151200074. Satoko and I get confused about this too.@k@vS06/07/151200075. 'Which color is broccoli and which is cauliflower...'``
changeReason: Old says 'Satoko can't tell the difference'; new says 'Satoko and I both get confused'; corrects who is confused.

TextENGNew: ``...You two siblings are absolutely priceless.``
According to Sis's intel, the dynamic was: unreliable big brother constantly nagged by his more competent little sister.
I'd never met his sister Satoko, but if her actual brother was like this, she must be at her wit's end.
Everything about Satoshi-kun's shopping was like this -- one thrill after another, start to finish.
``That's seasoned salt.``
changeReason: Corrects 'cooking salt' to 'seasoned salt' for 調理塩.

TextENGNew: ``...It's got pepper and other things mixed in. It's different from regular table salt.``
``Huh... really?@k@vS06/07/151200077. I think I remember separating salt and pepper in a science experiment once...``
``Never mind the excuses, just put it back.@k@vS06/06/150600408. ...This is plain salt, right here!@k@vS06/06/150600409. Okay, what's next?``
``Um... ahahahaha, having you here makes shopping so much easier, Mion...``
``...You look smart, Satoshi-kun, but you're seriously hopeless at daily life.@k@vS06/06/150600409_2. I really think you need more practice...``
``Mmph...@k@vS06/07/151200080. ...That's pretty harsh...``
``...Found it.@k@vS06/07/151200081. Is this the one?@k@vS06/07/151200082. In it goes.``
``Nooo!@k@vS06/06/150600414. That's wrong too!@k@vS06/06/150600415. Check the price and the serving size before you throw things in the basket!``
My first impression of Satoshi-kun had been of someone strong and dependable.
But the more time I spent talking with him, the more I was astonished by how helpless and precarious he was.
How do I put it... you just can't leave him alone.@kHe's a walking hazard.
If you let your guard down, he's the type who'd stand at a crosswalk wondering, 'Wait, do you cross on red?@kOr was it green?'@k...and get flattened by a dump truck... (sweatdrop)
``Your total comes to 1,780 yen.@k@vS06/00/psrtA15002. Do you have a stamp card?``
changeReason: Corrects 'member's card' to 'stamp card' for スタンプカード.

TextENGNew: ``Ah, sorry.@k@vS06/06/150600417. He can't find it right now, so we'll pass for today.``
changeReason: Old says 'old ladies'; new says 'battle-hardened housewives'; Japanese 海千山千 means experienced.

TextENGNew: Ignoring Satoshi-kun as he continued fishing through his receipt-stuffed wallet, I finished paying.
``That was mean, Mion...@k@vS06/07/151200087. You need to get stamps or it's a waste, you know...?``
``You don't go pawing through your wallet for a stamp card during the evening rush!@k@vS06/06/150600419. There was a huge line behind us!``

``Didn't you notice all those battle-hardened housewives glaring at us from behind, their eyes screaming 'hurry up already'?!@k@vS06/06/150600421. Listen -- when it's like that, you have your card ready before you reach the register!``
``...Oh... right.@k@vS06/07/151200089. Ahaha, that's good advice.``
``And it's not just when you're shopping.@k@vS06/06/150600423. Same goes for the bus!``

``Satoshi-kun, I bet when you get off the bus to pay the fare, that's the first time you even open your wallet and start picking through your change one coin at a time, making everyone behind you wait.``
``...Mmph... Well, everyone usually just walks past me and pays their own fare...``

``Haah...@k@vS06/06/150600426. You know what?@k@vS06/06/150600427. That is being an enormous nuisance to everyone around you!@k@vS06/06/150600428. You're supposed to have your money ready beforehand -- that's basic manners!@k@vS06/06/150600429. Understood?!``
``O-O-Okay...@k@vS06/07/151200092. ...You're scary today, Mion... Ahahaha...``
Satoshi-kun laughed nervously, as if trying to deflect my indignation.
...Hahhh... honestly, this guy.
...He was definitely the type who awakens a fierce protective instinct in people.
...Sure, when you've got a busybody like me around, he gets by just fine.@kBut I couldn't help worrying about whether he could function in society without someone watching over him.
We got plastic bags at the register and sorted out our purchases.
...When Satoshi-kun immediately shoved the tofu to the bottom of the bag, I smacked his hand with a sharp 'No!' and repacked it with the milk carton first.
``There. All good.@k@vS06/06/150600431. Now your aunt won't have a heart attack when she opens this bag in the kitchen.``
``Oh... thanks.@k@vS06/07/151200094. That really helped.@k@vS06/07/151200095. ...I'm not very good at the whole shopping thing.``
``Just shopping?@k@vS06/06/150600433. Be honest!``
``Ahahahahahaha!@k@vS06/06/150600435. Kidding, kidding.``
Brief as it was, our shopping trip had taught me a few things.
...It wasn't just that Satoshi-kun was bad at shopping.@k...He was fundamentally inept at practical matters in general.
...Spacey, you might say. Or absent-minded.
changeReason: Old says 'slow thinker'; new says 'spacey, absent-minded'; Japanese とろい means spacey.

TextENGNew: ...Thinking about it that way, the reckless courage he'd shown when we first met -- charging in heedless of danger -- must have been an extraordinary act of bravery for someone like him.
Satoshi-kun was spacey, but he wasn't stupid.
changeReason: Old says 'simpleton'; new says 'spacey'; Japanese とろい is not simpleton.

TextENGNew: He was fully aware that he was no good at this sort of thing.
...And precisely because of that, he should have been all the more inclined to practice the maxim 'a wise man steers clear of danger.'
changeReason: New preserves Japanese proverb about wise men avoiding danger.

TextENGNew: ...By rights, even if his classmate Mion was in trouble, he had no business charging in with reckless bravado.
He'd weighed all of that, forced it aside.@k...And leapt out anyway. That was something truly remarkable.
changeReason: Old says 'chose to be a man'; new says 'weighed it all, forced it aside, leapt out'; preserves deliberation.

TextENGNew: ``Good boy, good boy.@k@vS06/06/150600437. You did great, Satoshi-kun.@k@vS06/06/150600438. There, there.``
``Wh-... What are you doing?@k@vS06/07/151200098. Don't just start patting my head out of nowhere.``
``This is the 'Satoshi-kun Did His Shopping All By Himself' award.@k@vS06/06/150600440. Pat, pat, pat~``
``Mion... you're really making fun of me, aren't you?``
changeReason: New preserves playful Japanese award-naming style.

TextENGNew: ``Not at all!@k@vS06/06/150600442. Think you can make it home by yourself?@k@vS06/06/150600443. Do you know the way?``
``Mmph...@k@vS06/07/151200101. You really are making fun of me...``
Satoshi-kun pouted, but he let me keep patting his head without resistance, his face faintly reddening as he looked down.@k...Ahh, he's just too adorable.
Just then, a community bulletin board at the edge of my vision caught my eye.
Piano Lessons. Call XX.@k Abacus Class......@k Junior Baseball Team--Members Wanted.'
changeReason: Old says 'billboard'; new says 'community bulletin board'; Japanese 掲示板 is bulletin board.

TextENGNew: It was a recruitment poster for Satoshi-kun's team, the Hinamizawa Fighters.
changeReason: New adds team name from Japanese.

TextENGNew: It listed their weekly practice day and time, along with the location and other details.
changeReason: Old says 'next practice'; new says 'weekly practice day'; Japanese 毎週 means every week.

TextENGNew: If one shopping trip was enough to reveal him as this helpless...
...then even though I'd never seen it, his baseball must be much the same.
When Satoshi-kun noticed me reading the baseball team poster, he suddenly looked uncomfortable and tried to walk away.
``I know exactly what you just thought.@k@vS06/06/150600445. ...'Please, Mion.@k@vS06/06/150600446. Don't come watch me play baseball.'@k@vS06/06/150600447. ...Am I right?!``
``What's that 'mmph' supposed to mean?@k@vS06/06/150600449. ...That's a yes, isn't it.``
Satoshi-kun left behind a single, cryptic 'mmph' and fell silent.
...It seemed I'd hit the mark so squarely that he couldn't muster a single word in his defense.
...Satoshi-kun... there is such a thing as being too adorable, you know.
changeReason: Old says 'too cute'; new says 'there's a limit to being adorable'; Japanese ほどがある means there's a limit.

TextENGNew: ...The way he just went quiet and meekly conceded when I'd nailed it...@kWell, I suppose that's just how honest people are.
``See you later, Satoshi-kun!@k@vS06/06/150600451. It's getting dark, so be careful on your way home!``
``You're not heading back too?@k@vS06/07/151200106. To Hinamizawa?``
``...Oh, I've still got a stop to make.@k@vS06/06/150600453. Go on ahead without me.``
``You helped me with my shopping, so I'll tag along.@k@vS06/07/151200108. It's my turn to help.``

``Ah, ahahahaha.@k@vS06/06/150600455. I'm fine, really!@k@vS06/06/150600456. And what exactly are you planning to help with?@k@vS06/06/150600457. Maybe learn to shop on your own before you try helping other people.@k@vS06/06/150600458. Ahahahahahaha!``
``...Mmph...@k@vS06/07/151200110. You're being extra mean today, Mion...``
He did seem to have at least a shred of masculine pride, after all.
Being teased by a girl had him looking slightly indignant.@kNaturally, I was thoroughly enjoying myself.
Which is why, once again, I let my guard slip.
``Oh, but thanks for the help.``
``You really saved me.``
changeReason: Old says 'That was sweet'; new says 'You really saved me'; Japanese 助かった means saved/helped.

TextENGNew: With an innocent smile and those simple words, Satoshi-kun reached out and patted my completely unguarded head.@k...Pat, pat, pat, pat.
changeReason: New adds 'completely unguarded' matching Japanese まったく警戒していなかった.

TextENGNew: Ahh... I know, I know he's making fun of me, even so...@kUugh, while he's patting me I'm powerless to resist...@k...ngh...
Taking full advantage of my total lack of resistance, Satoshi-kun kept cheerfully patting away, seemingly content to do it forever.@k...auu auu auu...
``Well, see you, Mion.@k@vS06/07/151200114. Finish your errands before it gets dark.@k@vS06/07/151200115. ...See you tomorrow at school.``
``Oh, yeah!@k@vS06/06/150600462. See you tomorrow at school!``
The person who'd see him tomorrow at school wouldn't be me.
changeReason: Old says 'I wouldn't see him'; new says 'the person who'd see him wouldn't be me'; captures twin identity theme.

TextENGNew: ...There was a bittersweet twinge in that, but this was the best I could manage right now. It couldn't be helped.@kIf anything, I wanted to feel grateful to Sis for lending me her time as Mion.
After we parted ways...
...I noticed how incredibly happy I was, and how I was already yearning for the next time we'd meet.
I wanted to know more about him. So much more.
I could leave my next chance to see him up to chance.
